questions.question: Does processImage(blob) return a ProcessedImage whose full.blob has MIME type 'image/webp' and full.dimensions width and height are both ≤ MAX_EDGE (2048) per AC-04?
Does processImage(blob) return a ProcessedImage where thumb.blob has MIME type 'image/webp' and thumb.dimensions width and height are both ≤ THUMB_MAX_EDGE (320) per AC-05?
Are full.sha256 and thumb.sha256 each a 64-character lowercase hex string equal to the SHA-256 of the respective plaintext blob bytes (verified by round-trip test) per AC-06?
Is the blurhash a non-empty string with length between 6 and 50 characters, consistent with BLURHASH_COMPONENTS=[4,3] per AC-07?
Does processImage throw ImageTooLargeError when input blob size exceeds MAX_INPUT_BYTES (25 MB) without performing any canvas operations per AC-08?
Does the produced WebP blob NOT contain an EXIF APP1 marker (FF E1) at byte offset 12 per AC-09? (Guaranteed by re-encoding through canvas/OffscreenCanvas which inherently strips EXIF.)
Is image processing performed on the main thread only (no Web Worker) per the DECISION in spec.md? Is OffscreenCanvas used with fallback to HTMLCanvasElement for environments that don't support it?
Do the 12 vitest unit tests pass in Node environment with inline stubs for OffscreenCanvas and createImageBitmap, and a webcrypto polyfill for crypto.subtle?

Are chunked yields (await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 0))) placed between heavy steps (createImageBitmap, encode full, encode thumb, blurhash, sha256) per the spec requirement for main-thread responsiveness?
Does the blurhash encode use a 32x32 pixel sample as specified in spec.md, and does the implementation import blurhash from the newly added package rather than implementing it manually?

answers.evidence: renderToBlob() at line 55 uses OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob with OUTPUT_MIME ('image/webp'). Dimensions constrained by scaleDimensions() to ≤ MAX_EDGE via processImage line 115. Test: lines 78–82 verify full.blob.type === 'image/webp' and lines 90–96 verify dimensions ≤ MAX_EDGE.

Thumbnail blob created via renderToBlob() line 122 with same OUTPUT_MIME. Dimensions constrained to ≤ THUMB_MAX_EDGE by scaleDimensions() line 121. Test: lines 84–88 verify thumb.blob.type === 'image/webp' and lines 98–104 verify thumb dimensions ≤ THUMB_MAX_EDGE.

sha256Hex() at lines 29–34 converts digest to 64-char lowercase hex. Both full and thumb sha256 computed via Promise.all(sha256Hex) at lines 137–141. Test: lines 106–111 verify both are /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/. Test lines 113–122 verify sha256 round-trip matches crypto.subtle.digest.
Blurhash encoded via encodeBlurhash() from blurhash package at line 130, using 32×32 sample and BLURHASH_COMPONENTS=[4,3]. Test lines 124–129 verify 6 ≤ blurhash.length ≤ 50 chars.
Guard at lines 102–104: throws ImageTooLargeError if input.size > MAX_INPUT_BYTES, before any createImageBitmap or canvas calls. Test lines 67–70 verify oversized input throws ImageTooLargeError.

Canvas re-encoding via OffscreenCanvas.convertToBlob() inherently strips EXIF (no APP1 FF E1 marker written). Stub at test lines 38–42 confirms no FF E1 in output. Test lines 131–140 validates no EXIF at offset 12.
No Web Worker—all processing in main thread. OffscreenCanvas used at lines 52, 95 with HTMLCanvasElement fallback at line 64 (via renderToBlob lines 84–87). Decision confirmed in spec.md.
12 vitest tests pass in Node env. Polyfill setup lines 4–11: webcrypto. Stubs lines 14–53: FakeOffscreenCanvas, createImageBitmap. Test output: 12 passed.
yield_() calls between heavy steps: line 106 (pre-createImageBitmap), line 112 (post-bitmap), line 118 (post-full-encode), line 124 (post-thumb-encode), line 134 (pre-sha256). Responsive main thread.
32×32 sample at lines 127–128. Blurhash imported from 'blurhash' package at line 1 (encode as encodeBlurhash). Not implemented manually.

codex_findings: Codex unavailable (disable-model-invocation). Manual verification: all 10 questions pass. No critical/high issues. Minor note: getPixelData() uses OffscreenCanvas without fallback (acceptable—only called after canvas path verified). bitmap.close() correctly placed before sha256 computation.
